problem: A circle with a radius of 2 units has its center at $(0, 0)$. A circle with a radius of 7 units has its center at $(15, 0)$. A line tangent to both circles intersects the $x$-axis at $(x, 0)$ to the right of the origin. What is the value of $x$? Express your answer as a common fraction.

classified_questions.question: Can you describe how the tangent line intersects the circles in this scenario?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What do you notice about the right triangles formed by drawing radii to the tangent line?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Why are these two right triangles similar?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we set up a ratio using the lengths of the sides in these similar triangles?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you solve the equation formed by setting up this ratio to find the value of x?
classified_questions.label: b
